policies.name: full_disk_encryption
policies.description: FileVault (macOS), BitLocker (Windows), or LUKS (Linux) must be active. Unencrypted disks are a data breach waiting to happen if the device is lost or stolen.

policies.name: firewall_enabled
policies.description: The OS-level firewall must be active and blocking unsolicited inbound connections. Defense in depth even on managed networks.

policies.name: os_version_current
policies.description: The OS must be within one minor version of the latest stable release. Older versions miss security patches and widen the attack surface.

policies.name: ssh_key_only
policies.description: Password-based SSH must be disabled. Key-only auth eliminates brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks against remote access.

policies.name: gatekeeper_enabled
policies.description: macOS Gatekeeper must be active to verify app code signatures before execution. Prevents unsigned or tampered binaries from running.

policies.name: auditd_running
policies.description: auditd must be installed and active on Linux hosts. Without it, there is no kernel-level audit trail for security forensics.

policies.name: automatic_updates
policies.description: The OS automatic update mechanism must be enabled. Manual-only patching leads to drift and missed critical patches.

policies.name: screen_lock_timeout
policies.description: The screen must lock automatically after no more than 5 minutes of inactivity. Prevents unauthorized physical access to unlocked sessions.

policies.name: remote_login_disabled
policies.description: Remote Desktop / Screen Sharing / VNC must be disabled on end-user workstations. Reduces lateral movement surface if a credential is compromised.

policies.name: sip_enabled
policies.description: macOS SIP must be active. SIP prevents even root from modifying protected system files, a critical defense against persistent malware.

policies.name: bluetooth_sharing_disabled
policies.description: Bluetooth file sharing must be turned off. Reduces the wireless attack surface in shared office environments.

policies.name: guest_account_disabled
policies.description: The OS guest account must be disabled. Guest sessions can leave forensic gaps and bypass per-user audit trails.

policies.name: ntp_configured
policies.description: System clock must be synchronized via NTP. Unsynchronized clocks break certificate validation, Kerberos auth, and make forensic timelines unreliable.

policies.name: password_complexity
policies.description: Local account passwords must meet minimum length and complexity requirements. Weak local passwords are the first thing attackers try after initial access.

policies.name: usb_storage_restricted
policies.description: Removable USB storage must be restricted or monitored. Prevents both data exfiltration and malware introduction via physical media.

events: Bulk remediation: encryption + firewall push to 30 devices
New policy deployed: SSH key-only authentication
Patch Tuesday: macOS 15.4.1 pushed to all Macs
